title: ORISO Platform Management and Refactoring Quality

panels.managementQuestion: Can clients create an enquiry and can an eligible consultant see it?

beforeAfter.baseline: No causal live-chat success or failure signal exists; empty queues cannot distinguish no demand from a defect.
beforeAfter.target: Synthetic and real aggregate outcomes separate no demand, no eligible consultant, relation failure, and readback mismatch.

panels.managementQuestion: Do newly created organisations, counselling centres, relations, and users survive authoritative readback?

beforeAfter.baseline: Generic HTTP failures do not expose the failed provisioning stage or rollback result.
beforeAfter.target: Every create workflow records stage, success, authoritative readback, and compensation outcome without form values or identities.

panels.managementQuestion: Are all new rooms encrypted and are clients able to decrypt their events?

beforeAfter.baseline: Client decryption failures are counted, but successful encrypted-room creation is not measured end to end.
beforeAfter.target: 100% of successful room creations report encryption enabled; unencrypted creation is zero and blocks the gate.

panels.managementQuestion: Which service currently drives user-visible latency or errors?

beforeAfter.baseline: APM is available, but HTTP status classes and expected authentication failures are not yet separated in the management scorecard.
beforeAfter.target: Per-environment availability, p95 and p99 latency, and server-error SLOs are owned per service and exclude expected 4xx outcomes.

panels.managementQuestion: Can requests move between replicas without losing correctness or duplicating jobs?

beforeAfter.baseline: Workload kind Deployment is currently used as a proxy for statelessness despite known UserService local caches, maps, and schedulers.
beforeAfter.target: Critical local state count is zero, scheduled side effects are leader-safe or idempotent, and a multi-replica conformance test is green.

panels.managementQuestion: Are durable stores healthy, recoverable, and below capacity risk?

beforeAfter.baseline: Kubernetes and ClickHouse metrics exist; recovery objectives and complete Redis, Mongo, and Postgres saturation panels are not yet unified.
beforeAfter.target: Every stateful platform has availability, saturation, storage growth, backup age, and restore-test evidence in one scorecard.

panels.managementQuestion: Are PreDev and Dev running the reviewed commits and effective safety configuration?

beforeAfter.baseline: Runtime truth requires manual Kubernetes image and config inspection and has already shown stale Admin and UserService images.
beforeAfter.target: Every signal carries immutable commit, image digest, environment, E2EE flag, and availability-store configuration; drift is visible automatically.

panels.managementQuestion: Is missing data a product success, an idle system, or a broken telemetry pipeline?

beforeAfter.baseline: PreDev ingests metrics while Dev has no recent metrics; the dashboard cannot yet distinguish disabled exporters from collector failure at a glance.
beforeAfter.target: Environment heartbeat, accepted and rejected points, export failures, and last-seen timestamps make every telemetry gap explainable.
